patches.find: Non-discriminating: Chapman (1933) ionospheric mechanism predicts eclipse magnetic perturbations across all 39 eclipses in the ensemble. Dome adds no equation and triple-counts the same phenomenon as WIN-010, WIN-025, and WIN-068.
patches.replace: Non-discriminating: Chapman (1933) ionospheric mechanism predicts eclipse magnetic perturbations across all 39 eclipses in the ensemble. Dome adds no equation; this entry duplicates the observational basis already counted as WIN-010 and WIN-025 — the ensemble is a larger aggregation of the same phenomenon.
patches.rationale: WIN-068 listing itself in its own triple-count list is circular and confusing. Rephrased to clarify WIN-068 duplicates basis already counted in WIN-010 and WIN-025.

Meza et al. 2021 citation has no journal/volume/pages and cannot be verified after two review cycles. Removed per curmudgeon recommendation. Chapman 1933 alone is sufficient as the foundational reference.
Kim & Chang published 2018; V51.0 early 2026 = 8 years, not 9. Arithmetic error that a dome defender could exploit.

patches.find: For a separate internal-consistency concern targeting the dome's own mechanism (the refraction cascade makes the physical sun position seasonally indeterminate across the 39-eclipse ensemble), see
patches.replace: For a separate internal-consistency concern — the dome's refraction fix leaves the physical sun altitude indeterminate, undermining the geometric shadow it invokes for all 39 eclipses — see
patches.rationale: More self-contained phrasing that explains the concern without requiring the reader to already know Section 1.8. Removes the opaque 'seasonally indeterminate' phrase.
